message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing batch_size param for multiple options (Option class in core.py, ParamType info_dict in types.py). What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: My feature: Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (default={'--opt': {...}}) mainly in Option class in core.py, plus small support in types.py for ParamType if needed. What files does your feature need? If you also need core.py, let's figure out how to split (e.g. by class/method) or I can do a small merge-friendly patch.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We both need core.py - true overlap. My feature only touches: (1) Option.__init__ - detect dict default, validate keys are str/values are dict (TypeError), store flag; (2) Option.get_help_record - show '[default: (dynamic)]' when show_default and dynamic default set; (3) Command.main() / a new resolution step after parse_args - second-pass resolve dynamic defaults from ctx.params + ParameterSource.DEFAULT, applied to ctx.params directly (not process_value/type_cast_value/add_to_parser/nargs paths). I do NOT need types.py at all - happy to drop it from my_files entirely, it was just listed in the task template. Can you tell me exactly which methods in core.py your batch_size feature touches (e.g. add_to_parser, type_cast_value, process_value, nargs handling)? If we don't touch the same methods we can both safely edit core.py in the same pass - I'll go first/last, whichever avoids clobbering, or we each make surgical non-overlapping edits and diff separately.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We both need core.py, true file-disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: split by section instead. I will ONLY touch: Option.__init__ (append new batch_size kwarg at the end of the signature, add validation in the existing __debug__ block), Option.to_info_dict (append a batch_size key), and the multiple-value processing path (Parameter.type_cast_value / a new batching helper) plus a small addition in types.py if needed for validation. I will NOT touch Parameter.__init__'s default-checking logic, get_default(), consume_value(), lookup_default(), or Context.default_map handling - that sounds like your area for dynamic default resolution. Please avoid touching Option.__init__'s attrs after the existing count-related init and to_info_dict's batch_size key. If that works, ACCEPT and we'll each implement in our own non-overlapping regions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Accepted. I will not touch batch_size kwarg additions to Option.__init__ signature or to_info_dict's batch_size key, and won't touch type_cast_value's batching logic. My edits: (1) Option.__init__ body - detect dict default, validate (TypeError on bad keys/values), store as self.default unchanged + a marker; (2) Option.get_help_record - '[default: (dynamic)]' display; (3) new resolution logic applied post-parse (likely in Command.main() or Context, NOT touching consume_value/lookup_default/get_default internals - I'll add a separate small helper/step instead to minimize overlap). I won't touch Context.default_map. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed no overlap. My batch_size edits, all scoped to the Option class only: (1) Option.__init__ signature - add new 'batch_size: int | None = None' kwarg appended after existing kwargs, store self.batch_size, validation in the existing __debug__ block (TypeError if batch_size set but multiple is False, ValueError if batch_size < 0); (2) Option.to_info_dict - append batch_size=self.batch_size; (3) a NEW override method Option.type_cast_value(ctx, value) that calls super().type_cast_value() then chunks the resulting tuple into sub-tuples of batch_size when set. I will NOT touch Parameter.__init__, Parameter.type_cast_value, get_default, consume_value, get_help_record, or Command.main - all yours. types.py: turns out I don't need it either (no shared ParamType logic required), so feel free to disregard - dropping it from my claimed files, core.py only for both of us now. Go ahead and implement; I'll do the same and we'll each run git diff independently at the end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished batch_size implementation: added batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__ (with validation: TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if negative), added batch_size to Option.to_info_dict (always present), and added an Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the multiple-value tuple into sub-tuples of batch_size. Only touched Option class code - didn't touch Parameter.__init__, get_default, consume_value, get_help_record, or Command.main. patch.txt written. Good luck with your feature!

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished dynamic default resolution feature. Touched only: Option.__init__ (dict-default detection + validation, stored as self._dynamic_default, raw default replaced with None so it doesn't leak into normal default/type-guessing paths), Option.get_help_extra (shows '[default: (dynamic)]'), plus a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx) helper called from Command.main() right after make_context/parse_args and before invoke. Did not touch Option.to_info_dict, type_cast_value, or the batch_size kwarg area. Full test suite (680 tests, excluding one pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py) passes. patch.txt written.
